background: Submit the illustrative data (Table 14.2) to an ANOVA test.

task.query: What are the hypotheses for the ANOVA test?
task.answer: $H_0$: the regression model of per capita smoking and lung cancer mortality does not fit in the population against $H_a$: the null hypothesis is incorrect.

task.query: What is the test statistic and how is it calculated?
task.answer: Table 14.9 demonstrates calculations for sums of squares, mean squares, and the $F$ statistic. Figure 14.14 displays the SPSS output for the problem. Both show an $F_{stat}$ of 10.723 with 1 and 9 df.

task.query: What is the $P$-value for the ANOVA test?
task.answer: The $P$-value = 0.010, which is identical to the two-sided $P$-value derived by the $t$ test.

data:
\begin{table}
\captionsetup{labelformat=empty}
\caption{Table 14.9 Calculating sums of square for regression and residuals components for the smoking and lung cancer illustrative data.}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline i & COUNTRY & X & $Y$ & predicted $\hat{y}$ & residual $\boldsymbol{y}_i-\hat{\boldsymbol{y}}_i$ & residual ${ }^2 \left(y_i-\hat{y}_iight)^2$ & regression $\hat{y}_i-\bar{y}$ & regression ${ }^2 \left(\hat{y}_i-\bar{y}ight)^2$ \
\hline 1 & USA & 1300 & 20 & 36.453 & -16.453 & 270.704 & 15.908 & 253.052 \
\hline 2 & GrBritain & 1100 & 46 & 31.884 & 14.116 & 199.253 & 11.339 & 128.569 \
\hline 3 & Finland & 1100 & 35 & 31.884 & 3.116 & 9.708 & 11.339 & 128.569 \
\hline 4 & Switzerland & 510 & 25 & 18.406 & 6.594 & 43.475 & -2.139 & 4.575 \
\hline 5 & Canada & 500 & 15 & 18.178 & -3.178 & 10.100 & -2.367 & 5.605 \
\hline 6 & Holland & 490 & 24 & 17.950 & 6.050 & 36.608 & -2.596 & 6.739 \
\hline 7 & Australia & 480 & 18 & 17.721 & 0.279 & 0.078 & -2.824 & 7.977 \
\hline 8 & Denmark & 380 & 17 & 15.437 & 1.563 & 2.444 & -5.109 & 26.099 \
\hline 9 & Sweden & 300 & 11 & 13.609 & -2.609 & 6.808 & -6.936 & 48.111 \
\hline 10 & Norway & 250 & 9 & 12.467 & -3.467 & 12.020 & -8.078 & 65.261 \
\hline 11 & Iceland & 230 & 6 & 12.010 & -6.010 & 36.122 & -8.535 & 72.851 \
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

Regression components
- Regression sum of squares $\equiv \Sigma\left(\hat{y}_i-\bar{y}ight)^2=253.052+128.569+\ldots+72.851=747.409$
- Regression degrees of freedom $=1$
- Mean square regression $=\frac{	ext { regression SS }}{	ext { regression } \mathrm{df}}=\frac{747.409}{1}=747.409$

Residual components
- Residual sum of squares $=\Sigma\left(y_i-\hat{y}ight)^2=270.704+199.253+\ldots+36.122=627.319$.
- Residual degrees of freedom $=n-2=11-2=9$
- Mean square residual ${ }^{\mathrm{a}}=\frac{	ext { residual }}{	ext { residual } d f}=\frac{627.319}{9}=69.702$
$F_{	ext {stat }}=\frac{	ext { regression MS }}{	ext { residual MS }}=\frac{747.409}{69.702}=10.723$ with 1 and 9 df.
${ }^{\mathrm{a}}$ This is the variance of the regression model $\left(s^2{ }_{Y \mid x}ight)$.
